title: Peppery Silky Spanish Stir-fry
description: A solo-dinner Spanish dish featuring frozen seafood mix in a peppery and silky sauce suitable for nut-free diets.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: For cooking the frozen seafood mix

ingredients.name: Chili garlic sauce (optional for extra spice)
ingredients.notes: Can be omitted if not desired

instructions: Prepare the frozen seafood mix according to package instructions. Rinse and pat dry.
In a large skillet or wok, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Gently add the thawed frozen seafood mix (shrimp, mussels, clams) to the skillet. Use tongs or a slotted spoon to prevent overcrowding.
Sprinkle red pepper flakes evenly over the seafood and stir well to coat them with oil and spices.
If using chili garlic sauce, pour it in now and stir until fully incorporated into the mixture.
Pour in 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and let simmer for about 3-5 minutes or until seafood is cooked through and flavors are well melded.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course with crusty bread, olives, or a simple green salad.
